description: This file defines a WebSocket manager class for handling real-time broadcast of announcement events within a FastAPI application. It manages WebSocket connections, broadcasts messages, and handles client disconnects, all within a single process context.

classes.name: WebSocketClient
classes.description: A dataclass representing a connected WebSocket client, including the WebSocket object, user ID, and admin status.

classes.name: AnnouncementWSManager
classes.description: A class that manages WebSocket connections for broadcasting announcement events, with methods for connecting, disconnecting, and broadcasting messages.

methods.name: None __init__(self)
methods.description: Initializes the WebSocket manager with a lock and a set of clients.

methods.name: WebSocketClient connect(self, websocket: WebSocket, *, user_id: str, is_admin: bool)
methods.description: Accepts a WebSocket connection, creates a WebSocketClient, and adds it to the set of clients.

methods.name: None disconnect(self, client: WebSocketClient)
methods.description: Removes a client from the set of active clients.

methods.name: None broadcast(self, event: dict[str, Any])
methods.description: Sends a message to all connected clients, handling failures by dropping disconnected clients.

ctags.name: broadcast
ctags.pattern: /^    async def broadcast(self, event: dict[str, Any]) -> None:$/

ctags.name: connect
ctags.pattern: /^    async def connect(self, websocket: WebSocket, *, user_id: str, is_admin: bool) -> WebSocketC/
ctags.signature: (self, websocket: WebSocket, *, user_id: str, is_admin: bool)

ctags.name: disconnect
ctags.pattern: /^    async def disconnect(self, client: WebSocketClient) -> None:$/
